{{Infobox deity | Name = Ōtonoji no Kami | Generation = [[Kamiyo Nanayo|Fifth generation of Kamiyo Nanayo]] | Predecessor = [[Tsunugui no Kami]] | Successor = [[Omotaru no Kami]] | Deity_Type = [[Amatsukami]] | Full_Name = Ōtonoji no Kami (意富斗能地神) | Alternate = Ōtonomichi no Mikoto, Ōtomahiko no Mikoto, Ōtofu no Michi no Mikoto, Ōtomahiko no Mikoto | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * [[Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] * [[Futaarayama shrine]] }} | Mentioned = [[Kojiki]], [[Nihon Shoki]] | image= File:Ootonochi ootomabe shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of= {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|1=Kamiyonanayo}} | attributes= |associated_deities={{ill|Ōtonobe|WD=Q84868574|1=Ōtonobe}}; {{ill|Ōtonoji|WD=Q84868546|1=Ōtonoji}}}}
{{Infobox deity | Name = Ōtonobe no Kami | Generation = [[Kamiyo Nanayo|Fifth generation of Kamiyo Nanayo]] | Predecessor = [[Ikugui no Kami]] | Successor = [[Aya Kashikone no Kami]] | Deity_Type = [[Amatsukami]] | Full_Name = Ōtonobe no Kami (大斗乃弁神) | Alternate = Ōtonobe no Mikoto, Ōtomahime no Mikoto, Ōtofu no Michi no Mikoto | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * [[Futaarayama shrine]] }} | Mentioned = [[Kojiki]], [[Nihon Shoki]] }}
[[File:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|天地開闢 [//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/b5/Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg View as SVG]]]
'''Ōtonoji''' (意富斗能地, also known as '''Ōtonomichi''', 大戸之道) and '''Ōtonobe''' (大斗乃弁) are deities in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. They are also referred to as Ōtomahiko (大戸摩彦) and Ōtomahime (大戸摩姫).
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Ōtonoji no Kami is the elder brother, and Ōtonobe no Kami is the younger sister. In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, they are called Ōtonomichi no Mikoto and Ōtonobe no Mikoto, respectively. The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} refers to them as Ōtomahiko no Mikoto and Ōtomahime no Mikoto.
They are described as the fifth generation of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}}. Ōtonoji no Kami is a male deity, while Ōtonobe no Kami is a female deity.
The names symbolize the solidification of the earth. "Ōto" is interpreted in various ways, including as references to male and female generative organs, representing the earth’s readiness for life creation. Following these deities, Omotaru and Kashikone appear, signaling the world’s complete formation, with subsequent creation by {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}.
Ōtonoji is interpreted as "the great father at the village gate," while Ōtonobe signifies "the great woman at the village gate." These deities are believed to have been worshipped as guardians of gates in ancient settlements.
== Names == {| class="wikitable" ! Text !! Generation 1 !! Generation 2 !! Generation 3 !! Generation 4 !! Generation 5 !! Generation 6 !! Generation 7 |- | {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} (Male) || Umuji no Kami || Tsunugui no Kami || '''Ōtonoji no Kami''' || Omotaru no Kami || Izanagi no Kami || || |- | {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} (Female) || Suiji no Kami || Ikugui no Kami || '''Ōtonobe no Kami''' || Aya Kashikone no Kami || Izanami no Kami || || |- | {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} (Male) || Nidotsuchi || '''Ōtonomichi''' || Omotaru || Izanagi || || || |- | {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} (Female) || Satotsuchi || '''Ōtonobe''' || Kashikone || Izanami || || || |}
== Shrines == * {{ill|Enomiya Shrine|ja|宅宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enomiya Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|WD=Q207309|lt=Tokushima|1=Tokushima (city)}}, Kamiyachō) * {{ill|Hasuha Shrine|ja|波須波神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasuha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Prefecture, Izumo City, Sada Town, Shimohashiwa 172) * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} (Within the grounds: Shrine of the Kamiyo Nanayo generation, {{ill|Ōda, Shimane|en|Ōda, Shimane|WD=Q384796|lt=Ōda|1=Ōda, Shimane}}, Kawaimachi, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|WD=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine|1=Inbe Shrine}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, Higashi-Inbe Town, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Homisuwajūgosho Shrine|ja|穂見諏訪十五所神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homisuwajūgosho Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|WD=Q825272|lt=Hokuto, Yamanashi|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, Nagasaka Town) * {{ill|Futaarayama shrine|ja|二荒山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaarayama shrine|qq=}} (Within the grounds: Shrine of the Twelve Deities, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, Bamba Street, Tochigi Prefecture) * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shingū, Wakayama|en|Shingū, Wakayama|WD=Q823526|lt=Shingū|1=Shingū, Wakayama}}, Shingū) {{Kamiyonanayo}}
